Last year, Miami sold more homes for over $50 million than anywhere else in the country, while the rest of the market stalled. I wanted to know what was happening.
The richest titans of tech and finance, boldfaced names like Jeff Bezos of Amazon and Larry Page of Google, are buying waterfront compounds and competing for limited land.
There is a real sense of urgency, because there are so few prime parcels available.
Most of the deals are happening off market — on golf courses, country clubs and over text. Sometimes, buyers are cutting checks on the spot if a homeowner agrees.
When the buyers’ fortunes are so vast, prices soar into the stratosphere because, as one agent put it, $200 million “is just a rounding error.”
